layman_headline: Extreme fear grips crypto markets — patience is the best play right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' territory with a score of just 25 out of 100, and the mood is getting worse, not better. Bitcoin is holding around $64K but altcoins are getting hammered across the board. Most of the coins on our watchlist are in downtrends with bearish chart patterns. While fear often creates buying opportunities, the charts aren't yet showing signs of a bottom forming — we'd be catching a falling knife.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market hasn't shown a clear reversal signal yet. Watch for Ethereum to hold the $1,800 support level — if it bounces convincingly there with rising volume, that could be our entry point in the next day or two.
expert_reasoning: The current setup is a classic 'extreme fear' environment where contrarian entries can be highly profitable — but timing matters enormously. ETH's RSI at 39.4 is approaching but not yet at oversold (<30), and the chart bias score of -0.70 with price below all major MAs confirms the downtrend is still intact. The Three White Soldiers pattern on LDO (chart bias +0.85) is tempting, but with a 21% rally already in the bag and RSI at 59, the risk/reward ratio of 0.38x is poor for a new entry. BONK at RSI 31 is genuinely oversold, but the -2.00 chart bias with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu signals capitulation still in progress. The prudent move is to wait for ETH to test $1,800 support and see if buyers step in with conviction before committing capital.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.4% means capital is flowing out of alts — any BTC weakness would hit alts 2-3x harder
Fear & Greed at 25 and worsening suggests further downside before a true bottom forms
No clear MACD bullish crosses or volume breakout signals on any candidate — trend reversal not yet confirmed
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with volume would trigger a BUY signal next cycle
Fear & Greed index — stabilization or uptick from 25 would signal sentiment bottoming
LDO momentum — if it consolidates near $0.37-0.38 without giving back gains, a pullback entry toward $0.34 could emerge
market_context: The crypto market is in Extreme Fear (25/100, worsening) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.4%, signaling a risk-off rotation out of altcoins. BTC itself is relatively stable (+1.7% 24h) but altcoins are bleeding, particularly smaller caps like BONK (-26% weekly) and HYPE (-12% weekly).
context_banner: Extreme Fear markets eventually create the best swing entries — but discipline means waiting for the chart to confirm a bottom before committing capital.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 25 (Extreme Fear) and worsening from 27 yesterday — contrarian opportunity forming but not yet ripe for entry.
ETH RSI at 39.4 approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) but not yet triggered below 30.
'ETH derivatives reset and the next retail trade' — suggests positioning cleanup that could precede a bounce, supporting the ETH watchlist thesis.
FTX distributing $900M to creditors could add selling pressure short-term as recipients liquidate recovered crypto assets.
No candidates show bullish chart bias above +0.85, and the best-scoring candidate (LDO) has already rallied 21% with poor risk/reward ratio of 0.38x.
